description_long: This is a tall, narrow vase with a single curved handle and a flared, uneven rim. The surface is richly textured and features high-relief sculptural figures of dancing or prancing human forms, possibly inspired by mythological or Bacchic themes, surrounded by grapevines and foliage. The vase has a weathered, stone-like appearance with a predominantly grayish-blue patina, accented by lighter highlights on the raised figures and motifs. The handle is robust and textured, blending seamlessly into the body of the vase. The interior is visible at the mouth and appears to be a reddish-brown ceramic, contrasting with the exterior. The overall craftsmanship suggests an artistic, possibly antique or classical style, suitable for decorative display in a public or garden setting.
description: A tall, textured vase with sculpted mythological figures and grapevine motifs, finished in a weathered gray-blue patina with a single curved handle and a flared rim.
description_view_0: Front view shows a prominent sculpted figure in high relief, surrounded by grapevines and foliage, with the flared rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the robust, textured handle and a second sculpted figure, with more of the grapevine motif wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the handle's attachment and the heavily textured, less decorated surface, with minimal relief figures visible.
description_view_3: Side view displays another sculpted figure in motion, with the handle curving away and the grapevine motif continuing around the vase.
